task_text:
Fix `SECTION` nesting depth not reported in console reporter on failure

When an assertion fails inside a deeply nested `SECTION` in `internal/catch_section.cpp`, the console reporter in `reporters/catch_reporter_console.cpp` only prints the innermost section name. The full section path (parent > child > grandchild) should be displayed to help locate the failure.
solve_notes: Read `printTestCaseAndSectionHeader` and saw the loop calls `printHeaderString` once per section entry, producing one line per nested section. Changed the loop to accumulate a single path string joining section names with ` > `, then call `printHeaderString` once with the full path. `m_sectionStack[0]` is always the test case itself (pushed by RunContext); user sections start at index 1, so the `begin() + 1` skip is preserved.

search_sequence.result: Found the function at line 563; the inner loop calls `printHeaderString(it->name, 2)` once per sectionStack entry from index 1 onward
search_sequence.reasoning: Task description pointed directly to this function in this file

search_sequence.result: Declared at line 68 as `std::vector<SectionInfo> m_sectionStack`; `sectionStarting` pushes and `sectionEnded` pops entries
search_sequence.reasoning: Needed to understand element type and that index 0 is the test-case section, user sections start at 1

search_sequence.action: Read `printHeaderString` signature at line 598 of catch_reporter_console.cpp
search_sequence.result: Takes `std::string const& _string` and an optional indent; outputs a formatted header line
search_sequence.reasoning: Needed to know the correct signature for the single-call replacement

key_decisions.decision: Accumulate a single `std::string sectionPath` joined by ` > ` rather than returning a vector or using a stream
alternatives: recursive call that builds the header
key_decisions.reasoning: Simplest change that matches the existing `printHeaderString(std::string, indent)` signature; no allocation overhead beyond the single concatenation

exploration_log.aha_moment: Seeing that `m_sectionStack.begin() + 1` already skips the test-case entry confirmed the iteration bounds were correct and only the print strategy needed changing.
exploration_log.hindsight: Could have gone straight to `printTestCaseAndSectionHeader` without reading the whole streaming base; the field name was sufficient.

minimum_sufficient_defs.name: ConsoleReporter::printTestCaseAndSectionHeader
minimum_sufficient_defs.reason: edited: changed the section-display loop to accumulate one path string joined by ' > '

minimum_sufficient_defs.name: m_sectionStack
minimum_sufficient_defs.reason: read: needed to know element type (SectionInfo) and that index 0 is the test-case entry to correctly skip it

thrash_preventing_defs.name: ConsoleReporter::printHeaderString
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: must confirm `printHeaderString` accepts `std::string const&` so the accumulated path string compiles correctly; an agent without this would guess at the signature

thrash_preventing_defs.name: ConsoleReporter::printTestCaseAndSectionHeader
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: declaration in header; an agent scanning the class interface would inspect this before editing the .cpp

tier_difference_reasoning: minimum_sufficient covers the function being edited plus the field that tells us what the stack contains and how it is indexed. thrash_preventing adds `printHeaderString`'s full signature (so the agent doesn't have to guess whether it takes `std::string` or `StringRef`) and the class header declaration (which an agent would read before opening the .cpp implementation).

excluded_defs.name: RunContext::sectionStarting
excluded_defs.reason: confirmed that the first sectionStack entry is the test-case section, but this is already clear from the `begin() + 1` comment in printTestCaseAndSectionHeader itself

queries.query_text: When a test assertion fails inside deeply nested test sections the output only shows the innermost section name instead of the full path from root section to leaf section
queries.justification: Rule compliance: uses domain concepts 'test assertion', 'nested test sections', 'output', 'innermost section name', 'full path' — no symbol names or file paths. Pre-implementation: a developer seeing incorrect section nesting in test output would describe the symptom in these terms before knowing which reporter function to fix.

queries.query_text: "Skip first section (test case)" section path header
queries.justification: Rule compliance: 'Skip first section (test case)' is a literal comment in the original source that grep would find at the exact line being changed. Pre-implementation: a developer searching for 'skip first section' would land directly in the loop that iterates m_sectionStack and needs to be changed.

Rule compliance: lists 4 exact symbol names from the diff — the edited method, the container it iterates, the output method it calls, and the class owning them. Pre-implementation: a developer working on console reporter section display would start with these known symbol names.

Rule compliance: names concrete symbol `printHeaderString` and uses relationship word 'callers'. Pre-implementation: a developer wanting to understand how section headers are printed would ask which methods call printHeaderString.

Rule compliance: two explicit file paths from the solution. Pre-implementation: a developer fixing console reporter output would navigate to the console reporter implementation and its streaming base where m_sectionStack is declared.

queries.query_text: console reporter prints each nested section name on separate lines instead of building a single path string using printTestCaseAndSectionHeader and m_sectionStack
queries.justification: Rule compliance: mixes domain description 'prints each nested section name' with exact symbols 'printTestCaseAndSectionHeader' and 'm_sectionStack'. Pre-implementation: a developer who has identified the relevant method would refine their search by naming these symbols.
Rule compliance: two symbol names plus two file paths. Pre-implementation: a developer who has located the reporter files and identified the relevant symbols would combine both in a targeted search.

queries.query_text: Fix ConsoleReporter section nesting display: iterate m_sectionStack and build full path string with ' > ' separator in printTestCaseAndSectionHeader instead of printing each section individually
queries.justification: Rule compliance: includes symbol 'printTestCaseAndSectionHeader', 'm_sectionStack', file path 'catch_reporter_console.cpp', and domain context 'section nesting display'. Pre-implementation: this is the natural query a developer would type once they have identified the symptom and the relevant class.

test_selection.test_query: Find tests that verify the behavior of ConsoleReporter::printTestCaseAndSectionHeader in src/catch2/reporters/catch_reporter_console.cpp

relevant_preexisting_tests.reason: Has two levels of user sections (c -> d or c -> e) so m_sectionStack.size() > 1 is true and the section-path loop in printTestCaseAndSectionHeader executes
